title: Threadlight Production Readiness — waiver file
description: Schema for tests/production-readiness-waivers.json — customer-accepted findings with required justification and compensating control.

binding.description: OPTIONAL but STRONGLY RECOMMENDED in industrialized delivery. Anchors this waiver file to a specific customer + deployment + posture so waivers cannot silently leak between MVPs when teams copy repos. If absent, the skill applies waivers and emits a WAIVER-UNBOUND warning. If present, every populated field must match the current run; mismatch causes the file to be ignored entirely and a WAIVER-BINDING-MISMATCH warning fires. Empty-string fields are treated as 'any' (acceptable wildcard).

subscription_id.description: Azure subscription ID (GUID) the waivers were approved for. Compared against deployment_manifest.subscription_id.

resource_group.description: Resource group name the waivers were approved for. Compared against deployment_manifest.resource_group.

deployment_manifest_sha256.description: SHA256 over the post-deploy manifest's deployment_manifest block. Recompute with the value the skill prints in safe_check_ref.deployment_manifest_sha256.

target_posture.description: Posture these waivers were approved for. Compared against the resolved posture for this run.

customer_or_pilot_id.description: Free-form customer / pilot identifier for human auditability. Not enforced by the skill but helps reviewers see at a glance which MVP a waiver file belongs to.

approver_record.description: Free-form reference to the approval artifact (CR ticket, signed PDF, email thread).

id.description: Unique waiver ID within this file. Format: W-### (e.g., W-001).

finding_id.description: ID of the finding being waived. Must match a finding ID emitted by the skill (e.g., SEC-004, NET-501).

owner.description: Email of the person accountable for this waiver. NOT a distribution list.

expiry.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which the waiver is invalid. The skill ignores expired waivers and treats the underlying finding as un-waived.

justification.description: Why is this gap acceptable for the customer's risk posture? Free text, must be substantive (min 20 chars).

compensating_control.description: What offsetting control reduces the risk? Free text, must be substantive (min 20 chars).

accepted_risk.description: Plain-language summary of the residual risk being accepted. Free text, must be substantive (min 20 chars).

approver.description: Optional: name or role of the approver (e.g., 'CISO', 'Head of AI'). Defaults to owner if absent.

ticket_ref.description: Optional: reference to a ticket / change request (e.g., 'CR-12345').

raci.description: OPTIONAL governance fields for customers that audit waiver approvals against a RACI matrix. None of these change the skill's runtime behavior — they ride along on the manifest's `waivers_applied[*]` entries and surface in the report's waiver register table. Use these when the customer's CISO process requires explicit Accountable/Consulted/Informed roles distinct from the technical `owner`.

accountable_role.description: Role (not person) ultimately accountable for the residual risk — e.g., 'CISO', 'Head of AI Platform'. Distinct from `owner` (the technical accountable person) so a role rotation does not invalidate the waiver.

responsible.description: People or roles responsible for executing the compensating control. Free-form strings (emails or role names).

consulted.description: People or roles consulted before approval (e.g., legal, data protection, GRC). Free-form strings.

informed.description: People or roles informed of the waiver (e.g., audit committee, customer-side sponsor). Free-form strings.

waivers.description: List of accepted waivers. Each entry requires full justification + compensating control + accepted risk; otherwise the waiver is ignored and a WAIVER-INVALID finding is added to the report.
